Benchmark.title: STIG Baseline for Redis Container Image
Benchmark.description:
This STIG baseline template applies DISA Security Technical Implementation Guide controls to the Redis container image (Alpine Linux 3.19 with Redis 7.2.4 and wolfSSL FIPS).
Container Scope: - Application runtime security - Cryptographic enforcement - Audit logging - Access controls - Package management - Redis FIPS patch validation
Host Responsibilities (Out of Scope): - Kernel hardening - Boot process security - Physical security - Host network configuration - Host-level services

metadata.creator: Root Security Team
metadata.source: DISA STIG for Linux Systems (Alpine Linux adaptation)

Profile.title: Container FIPS Baseline
Profile.description: Baseline profile for FIPS-compliant containerized Redis on Alpine Linux 3.19. Includes only controls applicable to container runtime environments.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014
Group.description: The operating system must implement FIPS-validated cryptographic mechanisms.

Rule.title: Alpine Linux must use FIPS-validated cryptographic mechanisms.
Rule.description:
Use of weak or untested encryption algorithms undermines the purposes of utilizing encryption to protect data. The operating system must implement cryptographic modules adhering to the higher standards approved by the federal government.
Container Implementation: - wolfSSL FIPS v5.8.2 (Certificate #4718) - OpenSSL 3.3.0 with wolfProvider v1.1.0 - Redis 7.2.4 with FIPS patch (Lua SHA-256 hashing) - Alpine Linux 3.19 (musl libc 1.2.4)
Rule.check-content:
Verify FIPS mode is enabled in the container:
1. Check FIPS startup validation: $ docker run --rm cr.root.io/redis:7.2.4-alpine-fips fips-startup-check
Expected output: ✓ FIPS mode: ENABLED ✓ FIPS POST completed successfully ✓ wolfSSL FIPS module: OPERATIONAL ✓ FIPS 140-3 compliance: ACTIVE wolfSSL FIPS Certificate: #4718
2. Verify OpenSSL providers: $ docker exec <container> openssl list -providers
Expected output includes: wolfSSL Provider FIPS name: wolfSSL Provider FIPS version: 1.1.0 status: active
3. Verify Redis FIPS patch (Lua SHA-256): $ docker exec <container> redis-cli SCRIPT LOAD "return 'test'"
Expected: 64-character SHA-256 script ID (not 40-character SHA-1)
4. Verify redis.sha1hex() uses SHA-256: $ docker exec <container> redis-cli EVAL "return redis.sha1hex('test')" 0
Expected: 64-character SHA-256 hash
If FIPS providers are not configured, POST fails, or Lua SHA-256 is not working, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Ensure container is built with FIPS-validated cryptographic modules:
1. Use wolfSSL FIPS v5.8.2 (Certificate #4718) 2. Build OpenSSL 3.3.0 with wolfProvider v1.1.0 3. Apply Redis FIPS patch: redis-fips-sha256-redis7.2.4.patch - Replaces SHA-1 with SHA-256 for Lua script hashing - Modifies: src/eval.c, src/debug.c, src/script_lua.c, src/server.h 4. Compile fips-startup-check validation tool 5. Configure docker-entrypoint.sh to run FIPS validation on startup 6. Verify with diagnostic test suite (60+ tests)
Rebuild container if FIPS mode is not properly configured.

Rule.fixtext: Configure FIPS mode for the container

Group.title: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00015
Group.description: The operating system must block non-FIPS-approved cryptographic algorithms.

Rule.title: Alpine Linux must block non-FIPS cryptographic algorithms.
Rule.description:
The operating system must prevent the use of weak cryptographic algorithms that do not meet FIPS 140-3 requirements.
Container Implementation: - MD5 blocked at OpenSSL EVP API level - DES/3DES/RC4 blocked (not included in wolfSSL FIPS build) - SHA-1 replaced with SHA-256 in Redis FIPS patch - Lua scripts use SHA-256 (64-char IDs), not SHA-1 (40-char IDs) - redis.sha1hex() API uses SHA-256 internally
Rule.check-content:
Verify non-FIPS algorithms are blocked:
1. Test MD5 blocking: $ docker exec <container> openssl dgst -md5 /etc/redis/redis.conf
Expected: error:0308010C:digital envelope routines::unsupported
2. Test SHA-256 availability (FIPS-approved): $ docker exec <container> openssl dgst -sha256 /etc/redis/redis.conf
Expected: SHA256(...) = [hash]
3. Verify Lua SHA-256 hashing: $ docker exec <container> redis-cli SCRIPT LOAD "return 'Hello FIPS'"
Expected: 64-character SHA-256 script ID
4. Verify redis.sha1hex() uses SHA-256: $ docker exec <container> redis-cli EVAL "return redis.sha1hex('test')" 0
Expected: 9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08 (64 chars)
5. Run comprehensive algorithm tests: $ docker run -t --rm cr.root.io/redis:7.2.4-alpine-fips \ /diagnostics/test-images/basic-test-image/test-suite.sh
Expected: All tests passed, including: - TEST 3: FIPS enforcement (MD5 blocked) ✓ PASS - TEST 4: FIPS algorithm (SHA-256 working) ✓ PASS - TEST 9: Lua scripting (SHA-256 hashing) ✓ PASS - TEST 10: Lua redis.sha1hex() API ✓ PASS
If any non-FIPS algorithm is available or Lua SHA-256 is not working, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Ensure non-FIPS algorithms are blocked:
1. Build wolfSSL FIPS with only approved algorithms 2. Configure OpenSSL 3.3.0 to use wolfProvider exclusively 3. Apply Redis FIPS patch to replace SHA-1 with SHA-256 4. Verify MD5/DES/RC4 are not available 5. Test Lua script hashing returns 64-character SHA-256 IDs 6. Verify redis.sha1hex() returns 64-character SHA-256 hash 7. Run diagnostic test suite to validate enforcement
Rebuild container if non-FIPS algorithms are available.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000037-GPOS-00015
Group.description: The operating system must generate audit records for security events.

Rule.title: Alpine Linux must generate audit records for FIPS validation.
Rule.description:
The operating system must log security-relevant events to enable compliance monitoring and incident response.
Container Implementation: - docker-entrypoint.sh performs FIPS validation on startup - fips-startup-check executes FIPS POST and logs results - Diagnostic test suite provides comprehensive validation - Container logs include FIPS validation status - Fail-fast: Container terminates if FIPS validation fails
Rule.check-content:
Verify audit logging is configured:
1. Check entrypoint validation: $ cat /docker-entrypoint.sh
Expected: Contains FIPS validation logic and fips-startup-check execution
2. Review container logs: $ docker logs <container-id>
Expected output includes: - FIPS Startup Check output - FIPS POST validation - wolfSSL FIPS module status - OpenSSL provider verification - Redis startup messages
3. Verify diagnostic test suite: $ ls -la /diagnostics/
Expected: diagnostic.sh, test-redis-fips-status.sh, test-redis-connectivity.sh present
4. Run diagnostics: $ docker run --rm cr.root.io/redis:7.2.4-alpine-fips \ sh -c "cd /diagnostics && ./test-redis-fips-status.sh"
Expected: All FIPS validation tests pass
If audit logging is not configured or FIPS validation is not logged, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure audit logging:
1. Ensure docker-entrypoint.sh executes FIPS validation 2. Compile and include fips-startup-check binary 3. Configure container to log FIPS validation results 4. Implement fail-fast: exit on FIPS validation failure 5. Include diagnostic test suite in /diagnostics/ 6. Document all validation events in Evidence/ directory
Rebuild container with proper audit logging.

Rule.fixtext: Configure audit logging for FIPS validation

Group.title: SRG-OS-000067-GPOS-00035
Group.description: The operating system must verify package integrity.

Rule.title: Alpine Linux must verify package and library integrity.
Rule.description:
The operating system must validate the integrity of software packages and cryptographic libraries to prevent tampering.
Container Implementation: - wolfSSL FIPS integrity validated via fips-hash.sh during build - FIPS POST validates cryptographic module on every startup - SBOM generated in SPDX 2.3 format - VEX document for vulnerability disclosure - SLSA provenance for build attestation - Chain of Custody documentation - Comprehensive test suite: 60/60 tests passed
Rule.check-content:
Verify package integrity mechanisms:
1. Check FIPS POST execution: $ docker logs <container> 2>&1 | grep "FIPS POST"
Expected: "FIPS POST completed successfully"
2. Verify OpenSSL provider: $ docker exec <container> openssl list -providers
Expected: wolfSSL Provider FIPS v1.1.0 active
3. Check SBOM presence: $ ls compliance/SBOM-redis-7.2.4-alpine-fips.spdx.json
Expected: SPDX 2.3 format SBOM present
4. Check VEX document: $ ls compliance/vex-redis-7.2.4-alpine-fips.json
Expected: OpenVEX format VEX document present
5. Check SLSA provenance: $ ls compliance/slsa-provenance-redis-7.2.4-alpine-fips.json
Expected: SLSA v1.0 provenance present
6. Verify test suite results: $ cat Evidence/test-execution-summary.md
Expected: 60/60 tests passed (100%)
If package integrity is not verified or tests failed, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure package integrity verification:
1. Execute fips-hash.sh during wolfSSL build 2. Run FIPS POST on every container startup 3. Generate SBOM in SPDX 2.3 format 4. Create VEX document for vulnerability disclosure 5. Generate SLSA provenance for build attestation 6. Document Chain of Custody in compliance/CHAIN-OF-CUSTODY.md 7. Run comprehensive test suite and document results 8. Store evidence in Evidence/ directory
Rebuild container with integrity verification.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000095-GPOS-00049
Group.description: The operating system must enforce least privilege via non-root user.

Rule.title: Alpine Linux must run Redis as a non-root user.
Rule.description:
The operating system must prevent unnecessary privilege escalation by running applications as non-root users.
Container Implementation: - Redis runs as redis user (UID 1000, GID 1000) - /data directory owned by redis:redis - USER redis directive in Dockerfile - No sudo or privilege escalation mechanisms
Rule.check-content:
Verify Redis runs as non-root:
1. Check running processes: $ docker exec <container> ps aux | grep redis
Expected: Redis process running as redis user (UID 1000)
2. Verify user at runtime: $ docker run --rm cr.root.io/redis:7.2.4-alpine-fips id
Expected: uid=1000(redis) gid=1000(redis) groups=1000(redis)
3. Check Dockerfile USER directive: $ docker inspect cr.root.io/redis:7.2.4-alpine-fips | grep User
Expected: "User": "redis" or "User": "1000"
4. Verify data directory ownership: $ docker exec <container> ls -ld /data
Expected: drwxr-xr-x ... redis redis ... /data
If Redis runs as root or UID 0, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure non-root user:
1. Create redis user and group (UID 1000, GID 1000) 2. Set USER redis in Dockerfile 3. Ensure /data is owned by redis:redis 4. Verify no sudo or setuid binaries are present 5. Test Redis startup as redis user
Rebuild container with non-root user configuration.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000096-GPOS-00050
Group.description: The operating system must restrict file permissions.

Rule.title: Alpine Linux must restrict file permissions to prevent unauthorized access.
Rule.description:
The operating system must set appropriate permissions on files to prevent unauthorized modification or disclosure.
Container Implementation: - No world-writable files - Libraries: 0755 (wolfSSL, OpenSSL) - Configuration files: 0644 (redis.conf) - Scripts: 0755 (docker-entrypoint.sh, fips-startup-check) - Data directory: 0755, owned by redis:redis
Rule.check-content:
Verify file permissions:
1. Find world-writable files: $ docker exec <container> find / -type f -perm -002 2>/dev/null
Expected: No output (no world-writable files)
2. Check sensitive files: $ docker exec <container> ls -la /usr/local/lib/libwolfssl.so* $ docker exec <container> ls -la /usr/local/openssl/lib/libssl.so* $ docker exec <container> ls -la /etc/redis/redis.conf $ docker exec <container> ls -la /docker-entrypoint.sh $ docker exec <container> ls -la /usr/local/bin/fips-startup-check
Expected permissions: - Libraries: -rwxr-xr-x (0755) - redis.conf: -rw-r--r-- (0644) - Scripts: -rwxr-xr-x (0755)
3. Check data directory: $ docker exec <container> ls -ld /data
Expected: drwxr-xr-x ... redis redis ... /data
If any world-writable files exist or permissions are incorrect, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Set proper file permissions:
1. Remove world-writable permissions: find / -type f -perm -002 -exec chmod o-w {} \; 2. Set library permissions: chmod 0755 /usr/local/lib/*.so* 3. Set config permissions: chmod 0644 /etc/redis/redis.conf 4. Set script permissions: chmod 0755 /docker-entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/fips-startup-check 5. Set data directory: chown redis:redis /data && chmod 0755 /data
Rebuild container with correct file permissions.

Rule.title: Kernel module loading (Not Applicable - Host Responsibility)
Rule.description:
This control applies to the host kernel, not the container. Containers share the host kernel and cannot independently load kernel modules.
Justification: Host responsibility Mitigation: Deploy container on STIG-compliant host
Rule.check-content: This control is not applicable to containers. Verify host kernel is STIG-compliant.

fix.fixtext: Not applicable to container. Harden host kernel per DISA STIG.

Rule.title: Boot loader password (Not Applicable - No Boot Process)
Rule.description:
This control applies to boot loaders (GRUB, etc.). Containers don't boot - they are started by container runtime.
Justification: Containers have no boot loader Mitigation: N/A
Rule.check-content: This control is not applicable to containers. Containers don't have boot loaders.

fix.fixtext: Not applicable to container. Containers are started by runtime, not booted.

Rule.title: Systemd service hardening (Not Applicable - No Systemd)
Rule.description:
This control applies to systemd service management. Alpine Linux uses OpenRC, not systemd. Minimal containers use entrypoint scripts.
Justification: Alpine Linux doesn't use systemd Mitigation: Process supervision by container runtime
Rule.check-content: This control is not applicable to Alpine Linux containers. Alpine uses OpenRC, minimal containers use entrypoint scripts.

fix.fixtext: Not applicable to Alpine Linux. Process managed by docker-entrypoint.sh.
